title: chaerophyllum hirsutum var. villarsii
intro: Chaerophyllum hirsutum var. villarsii, also known as rough-chervil, is a perennial plant in the parsley family (Apiaceae) native to the mountainous regions of Europe. It has been used for centuries in traditional medicine, valued for its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and carminative properties. The plant is said to offer various health benefits, including relief from digestive issues, colds, and respiratory problems, as well as acting as a natural expectorant. Chaerophyllum hirsutum var. villarsii can be prepared as an herbal tea, infusion, or tincture, making it a popular ingredient in natural remedies.
